title: dryopteris submarginalis var. lagerheimii
intro: Dryopteris submarginalis var. lagerheimii, also known as the Japanese Wood Fern, is a type of fern that belongs to the Dryopteridaceae family. This plant is used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat various health conditions such as skin irritations, wounds, and respiratory issues. The medicinal properties of Dryopteris submarginalis var. lagerheimii can also help to reduce fever, relieve digestive issues, and promote overall well-being. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal benefits.
